Modes "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection
SubSection "Display"
Depth 24
Modes "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
EndSubSection
EndSection

### Post by pistcivet on 2007-01-23
change:
VertRefresh 50.0 - 160.0
to:
VertRefresh 100.0 - 160.0

can be a range or a comma separated list.

---

### Post by clown99 on 2007-01-23
Thanks for the idea. But thats not the solution :(.

### Post by pistcivet on 2007-01-23
Should work.
Did you restart the x-server after you made the change? (Ctrl-Alt-Backspace)

Editing /etc/X11/xorg.conf is the only way I know of to change refresh frequencies.

Why is 85Hz not acceptable?

### Post by clown99 on 2007-01-25
Well.

First and standard-nonsoftware way. I use the screen-menu. It shows me the resoulation and hz.

---

### Post by david_2001 on 2007-01-25
Chances are that your monitor is designed to work best at 85Hz, regardless of the maximum possible refresh rate.  Xorg will tend to stick to the "optimal" preset refresh rates unless a custom ModeLine is used.  Of course, Windows may allow a higher refresh rate to be forced, in the same way that X can be set-up to totally destroy a monitor; however, this doesn't mean that the performance will be better 8-).

TTFN

---

### Post by meng on 2007-01-25
> **abhi.944 said:**
> I have a similiar problem .. u see i had recently switched to ubuntu .. earlier i ws on FC6, now in ubuntu i cn't get a resolution above 1024 x 768, though i wanted to work on 1280 x 1024 like i used to work on FC6. In the screen resolution dialog there is no option for 1280 x 1024. I tried even manipulating xorg.conf but no help. 

Could u think some of you might be of any help !!!
Best you start a new thread since it's not really a similar problem.
You could, however, try:
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
and select the resolution/s you want.
Then restart the X server.

---

### Post by meng on 2007-01-25
To the original poster:
85 Hz vertical refresh rate is just as good as 100 Hz. Below 75 Hz one may experience eyestrain, but above a certain level it shouldn't make a difference - unless you are especially sensitive to it. More is not necessarily better.

Just my opinion, of course.
